div.h1: BPF Verifier: Abstract Interpretation & Path Exploration
div.p: How the kernel mathematically proves eBPF safety before execution

g.text: All loops have provable upper bound
All map lookups checked for NULL

div.p: 0: (b7) r1 = 0 # i = 0 1: (b7) r0 = 0 # sum = 0 2: (bf) r2 = r1 # loop check 3: (15) if r2 == 1000 goto +4 # branch 4: (0f) r0 += r1 # sum += i 5: (07) r1 += 1 # i++ 6: (05) goto -5 # loop back 7: (95) exit # return sum

g.text: R2 | scalar | range: [0, 999] | copy of R1

div.p: Iter 1: R1 ∈ [0, 999] → continue Iter 10: R1 ∈ [9, 999] → continue Iter 100: R1 ∈ [99, 999] → continue Iter 500: R1 ∈ [499, 999] → continue ... PROBLEM: Verifier cannot prove:

ul.li: Loop will terminate The 'while' condition depends on runtime value, not compile-time constant.

div.p: Error: infinite loop detected last idx 6, cur idx 2 2: (bf) r2 = r1 3: (15) if r2 == 1000 goto pc+4 Hint: Use bounded loop with constant: #pragma unroll for (int i = 0; i < 100; i++) { }
Inst 3: r2 != 1000 → goto inst 4 → inst 5 (i++) → inst 6 (goto -5) → back to inst 2 ITERATION 1: r1 ∈ [0,999] ITERATION 2: r1 ∈ [1,999] ... ITERATION N: r1 ∈ [N,999]
Inst 3: r2 == 1000 → goto inst 7 → exit PROVABLE: terminates when r1 reaches 1000
